'''''Star Wars'' 21''' is the twenty-first issue in the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of comics. It was published on [[August 23]], [[2000]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Aayla Secura is the Padawan apprentice of the mysterious Jedi Quinlan Vos, and Vos needs to know why she disappeared -- and that knowledge is trapped in the dark recesses of his lost memory. And as Vos searches for answers, he may not like what he finds, as those who appear to be friends turn out instead to be lethal enemies. Join Quinlan in his deadly quest for the truth in the third chapter of "Twilight."''

==Plot summary==
As they arrive on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]], [[Vilmarh Grahrk]] and [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] encounter one of Quin's clansmen, who takes him to his lost Padawan's [[Pol Secura|uncle]]. Meanwhile, Villie meets with his cousin [[Holmar Grahrk|Holmar]] to discuss their plot to betray the Jedi. After Secura shows the Jedi to his room, he receives a communiqué from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and a mysterious patron commands Secura to kill Vos. During a conversation with his fellow Kiffar, Quin receives a transmission from Villie, asking him to meet him in the [[Floating Rock Gardens of Ryloth|Rock Gardens]]. Upon arriving, Quin is attacked by Holmar Grahrk, who is killed in a betrayal by his cousin. As it turns out, Villie has placed another bet on Quin's survival, using his cousin as a pawn. Continuing his search for Aayla, Quin heads into an "abandoned" [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]] mine, accompanied by Grahrk. Unexpectedly, they are attacked by [[energy spider]]s, imported from [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] to assist in the creation of [[glitteryll]]. As the spiders overwhelm them, Quin is shot in the back by [[Asanté Vos]]. Asanté explains her involvement in the plot to wipe Quin and Aayla's minds as an attempt to save them from assassination as they got too close in their investigation of the glitteryll. After Villie kills Asanté, the unlikely pair head off in search of Pol Secura once again.
